matches.match_rationale: The core pain is a multi-step, recurring workflow: gather supporting docs from campus finance teams, validate completeness per program requirements, reformat to submission templates, and submit by deadline — repeated 12+ times per year across 4 programs. Task automation agents can orchestrate this end-to-end: triggering collection reminders, routing docs through validation checkpoints, flagging missing items, and populating submission forms across programs. This directly addresses the >40% time spent on format-converting and chasing missing docs.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated compliance pipeline that, for each program-cycle (e.g., DepEd ESC Q2), triggers campus finance teams to upload source documents, validates completeness against a program-specific checklist, reformats attachments to the required template, and surfaces a submission-ready package — replacing the current manual coordination loop.

matches.match_rationale: The pain description explicitly notes that a missed deadline in one program cascades to another (DepEd rejecting a GASTPE claim due to stale status). A risk-scoring model tracking document completeness, days-to-deadline, campus submission lag history, and cross-program dependency chains could flag impending deadline misses before they occur — giving compliance staff a prioritized intervention list rather than discovering failures after submission rejection.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance risk dashboard that scores each open submission cycle by probability of on-time completion, surfacing at-risk cycles with specific gap analysis (e.g., Campus 2 GASTPE Q3: beneficiary list missing, 4 days to deadline, historically 3-day lag) — enabling the CFO to redirect staff before a fund hold occurs.

matches.match_rationale: Each of the 4 programs (DepEd ESC, GASTPE, LGU grants, SUC subsidies) requires the same underlying financial data in incompatible formats — different column orders, label conventions, and aggregation levels. Compliance staff currently re-key or manually reformat the same payroll registers, receipt logs, and beneficiary lists into each program template. AI-assisted schema mapping can normalize source data from the operator internal records and emit program-specific outputs automatically.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A format-normalization layer that ingests campus-level payroll registers and receipt logs in whatever internal format they exist, then outputs DepEd-formatted teacher rosters, GASTPE liquidation templates, and LGU acquittal sheets — eliminating the manual reformatting that consumes the largest share of compliance staff time.

matches.match_rationale: Government-issued supporting documents — receipts, LGU acknowledgment letters, official payroll printouts — often arrive as scanned PDFs or image files. Before any transformation or validation can occur, these must be digitized into machine-readable data. OCR and form-field extraction directly enables the upstream intake step for the automation pipeline, and reduces the manual data-entry burden when auditors request specific historical evidence such as a Q3 teacher attendance log.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An intake processor that accepts scanned receipts, payroll registers, and beneficiary lists via upload, extracts key fields (amounts, dates, names, program codes) into a structured record, and tags each document to its program and submission cycle — creating a searchable, audit-ready repository.

matches.match_rationale: Compliance staff and CFOs frequently need answers to program-specific procedural questions — what supporting documents does GASTPE require for semi-annual liquidation, or what is the retention period for DepEd ESC audit files — currently answered by digging through program memoranda and prior-year submission records. A RAG system over indexed program guidelines and historical submissions would eliminate lookup time and reduce the risk of missing a changed requirement.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance knowledge assistant that indexes DepEd, GASTPE, and LGU program circulars alongside prior-year submission packages, enabling compliance staff to ask plain-language questions and receive sourced, current answers — reducing time spent interpreting conflicting agency guidance.
